commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because this draft collaborator PR changes the runtime plugin support contract and overlaps https://github.com/openclaw/nix-openclaw/pull/100; there is no narrow automated repai...; Cleared: The diff adds supply-chain-sensitive materialization paths, but the reviewed implementation records fixed tarball hashes, npmDepsHash values, and integrity checks with no concrete unpinn... (sha=a7b84fb8a5cefec9c6ab73710d3316c0717ca4ca)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shrinkwrap-backed and ClawHub-resolved runtime plugin materialization, regenerates supported plugin locks, and updates runtime-plugin documentation and checks.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a bug report. The relevant verification is build/materialization proof for shrinkwrapped runtime plugins, which the PR body provides for the final head.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because this draft collaborator PR changes the runtime plugin support contract and overlaps https://github.com/openclaw/nix-openclaw/pull/100; there is no narrow automated repair finding.

target.title: feat: materialize shrinkwrapped runtime plugins
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## Maintainer Decisions

This PR keeps the user API simple: users enable supported OpenClaw runtime plugin ids with `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "slack" "copilot" ];`. They do not pass npm, ClawHub, git, archive, or local-path install strings in Home Manager config.

The packageability rule is also simple: nix-openclaw can expose an upstream plugin when the selected artifact can be built reproducibly as a fixed Nix input. Today that means no runtime dependencies, bundled dependencies, or a package-local `npm-shrinkwrap.json` that lets Nix materialize dependencies with an `npmDepsHash`.

## Changes

- Adds shrinkwrap-backed npm dependency materialization for OpenClaw runtime plugin roots.
- Adds ClawHub artifact resolution at lock-generation time, then builds the resolved artifact through the same immutable Nix path.
- Expands the generated supported runtime plugin set for the pinned OpenClaw source.
- Rewrites the README runtime plugin section for end users using familiar examples: Slack for channel plugins and GitHub Copilot for runtime/provider plugins.
- Keeps maintainer diagnostics in the generated report instead of turning skip reasons into user-facing API concepts.

## User-Facing Support Contract

Upstream install commands map to supported ids:

| Upstream OpenClaw | nix-openclaw |
| --- | --- |
| `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/slack` | `runtimePlugins = [ "slack" ];` |
| `openclaw plugins install clawhub:@openclaw/whatsapp` | `runtimePlugins = [ "whatsapp" ];` |
| `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/copilot` | `runtimePlugins = [ "copilot" ];` |

Direct arbitrary source specs are not exposed as user config in this PR. If we add that later, it should be a separate Nix package definition with explicit source and dependency hashes, not `openclaw plugins install` at activation time.

## Current Packageability Gaps

The current OpenClaw 2026.6.1 generated report leaves Weixin, Yuanbao, and WeCom out because their published artifacts declare runtime npm dependencies without `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and without bundled `node_modules`. A plugin-side shrinkwrap or bundled dependency publication should make those packageable by this pipeline.

The remaining PixVerse skipped row is a duplicate upstream row, not a missing packageability class.

## Proof

Latest head: `a7b84fb8a`.

Final-head local proof:

- `git diff --check`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-plugin-locks`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci`

Earlier implementation proof on this branch also built representative generated plugin packages for Slack, WhatsApp, Matrix, Codex, ACPX, and Memory LanceDB.

## Merge Notes

This branch overlaps PR #100 at the broader shrinkwrap packaging layer. Merge order should be settled before landing, then remote CI/Garnix should be green for the exact commit merged.
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
